must_contain_any: // Catch a lockfile where the union of fork markers doesn't cover the supported
notes: Verified: 'impl Lock' and the lock/mod.rs file guess both correct. IMPORTANT COLLISION for sentinel: crates/uv-resolver/src/lock/export/pylock_toml.rs:959-960 defines an UNRELATED PylockToml::to_toml with the byte-identical signature 'pub fn to_toml(&self) -> Result<String, toml_edit::ser::Error> {' and the byte-identical doc comment 'Returns the TOML representation of this lockfile.' -- both were dropped from must_contain_any to avoid crediting either arm for a false-positive hit on the wrong type. The two body-unique lines chosen instead were verified present only in the true Lock::to_toml body.

Confirmed real dead end: 'grep -rn Resovler crates/' returns zero matches (the internal 'lv'->'vl' transposition breaks substring containment) -- genuine grep-dead-end case as intended, unlike django's T04 trailing-truncation. IMPORTANT FOR SENTINEL: the canonical_symbol_guess 'Resolver.solve' does NOT exist verbatim anywhere in the fixture. The real PubGrub-driving method (the one that 'actually runs the algorithm', per the query) is the PRIVATE 'fn solve' on ResolverState at resolver/mod.rs:313-746 (430 lines -- the core solver loop, spawned on a dedicated thread). The public entry point an outside caller actually invokes is the differently-named 'pub async fn resolve(self) -> Result<ResolverOutput, ResolveError>' on Resolver at resolver/mod.rs:276-309, which spawns the thread and awaits ResolverState::solve -- an adjacent, also-plausible answer to the query's 'returns the resolution graph' half. Gold is anchored on ResolverState::solve as the closer match to 'actually runs the algorithm'; Resolver::resolve is documented here as the adjacent orchestration wrapper, not included in must_contain_any. Bash step 2 ('grep -rn --include=*.rs "fn solve" crates/uv-resolver/src/resolver/', no context flags) does capture the bare 'fn solve(' signature line as a real, single-file-scoped, globally-unique hit.

notes: Confirmed real dead end: 'grep -rn PythonInstalation crates/' (missing internal 'l') returns zero matches -- genuine grep-dead-end case, internal-typo style as recommended after the django curation. PythonInstallation::find and the installation.rs file guess are both exactly correct. IMPORTANT COLLISION for sentinel: crates/uv-python/src/environment.rs:152 defines PythonEnvironment::find with the byte-identical 4-space-indented signature line 'pub fn find(' (even cross-referenced by PythonInstallation::find's own doc comment: 'If looking for a Python interpreter to create a new environment, use [`PythonInstallation::find`] instead' appears on environment.rs, not here) -- the bare signature snippet was excluded from must_contain_any to avoid a false-positive hit credited to the wrong type, in favor of two body-unique lines. STRUCTURAL NOTE: bash step 2 as pre-registered ('grep -rn --include=*.rs "fn find" crates/uv-python/src/installation.rs', no -A/-B context flags -- mirroring django T04/T05's own no-context style) can only ever print the single matched line per hit; because Rust's rustfmt convention wraps multi-parameter signatures across several lines (unlike Python's single-line def), the recipe's captured text is 'pub fn find(' alone and can never reach either body-unique gold snippet -- an honest, structural bash loss on this task, not a curation gap, and not something the pre-registered recipe can be edited to fix post-hoc.

must_contain_any: A cache entry is considered fresh if it was created after the cache itself was
notes: Confirmed real and cleanly resolved: Cache::freshness (uv-cache/src/lib.rs:337-377) is exactly the staleness/reuse decision the query paraphrases -- compares a cache entry's on-disk timestamp against the Refresh policy's cutoff and returns Fresh/Stale/Missing. The recipe's step 2 keyword guess ('freshness') hits the method name directly; unlike django's T06 paraphrase-gap case, this one is not adjacent-but-wrong -- it is the real decision function.

Confirmed real: Error::hash_mismatch (uv-distribution/src/error.rs:244-296) is constructed and raised from crates/uv-distribution/src/source/mod.rs at 10+ call sites (e.g. line 647), each preceded by the identical comment '// Before running the build, check that the hashes match.' and guarded by 'if !revision.satisfies(hashes) {'. The 10x repetition of that guard comment across source/mod.rs is a genuine multiplicity of correct positive occurrences (the same real check repeated per distribution-fetch code path), not a false-positive collision with an unrelated concept -- noted for sentinel but not treated as noise to curate away.

CORRECTION to the bash_recipe_rationale's assumption: no explicit 'uv collects no telemetry' denial statement was found in README.md or docs/ either -- the reality is total silence on the topic in the fixture, not a documented denial as the pre-registered rationale assumed. The core claim (uv implements no telemetry/analytics collection) still holds and is the basis for expect_absence=true.

must_contain_any: /// Make a "Simple API" request for the package and convert the result to a [`VersionMap`].
notes: MAJOR DISCREPANCY -- FLAGGING PROMINENTLY FOR SENTINEL. The task's pre-registered symbol field 'RegistryClient.simple' does not exist anywhere in the fixture: RegistryClient has no bare simple() method (confirmed: 'grep -rn \.simple\( crates/' returns zero matches). The real method is RegistryClient::simple_detail (crates/uv-client/src/registry_client.rs:327), called from four sites: uv-resolver's resolver/provider.rs:181 (inside DefaultResolverProvider::get_package_versions, matching the bash_recipe_rationale's own guess that the resolver is the primary caller), uv-audit/src/service/project_status.rs:69, uv-publish/src/lib.rs:969, and uv/src/commands/pip/latest.rs:50. CONSEQUENCE FOR SCORING: archetype 6's ARCHETYPE_PLAN is trace(primary) -> locate(followup_trace_callers), and the followup step is gated on the trace step NOT reporting empty ('if prev_empty or not calls: continue'). A trace call for a symbol that does not exist will hit vectr's own honest no-symbol banner, prev_empty becomes True, and the followup locate step is skipped entirely -- the vectr arm would score an unconditional, structural miss on T09 caused by this blind naming error, not by any real retrieval weakness. This task's symbol field, as literally pre-registered, cannot be corrected without touching benchmarks/vs_bash/tasks_uv.jsonl (which the two-commit honesty rule forbids editing post-commit). Recommend sentinel either (a) score T09 as-is and report the resulting miss with this note attached (an honest data point about naming-guess fragility, in the spirit of archetype 3 though this task is categorized as archetype 6), or (b) exclude T09 from the scored set and treat it as a known-compromised task pending a corrected re-registration in a future task-set revision.

Confirmed real and rich: create_venv (crates/uv-virtualenv/src/lib.rs:76, a bare free function) is called from 13 real sites across the workspace, including crates/uv/src/commands/venv.rs:200 (inside the venv() command handler) and crates/uv/src/commands/project/run.rs:482 (inside the run() command handler, one of four call sites in that single file), plus commands/project/mod.rs (x4), commands/project/environment.rs, uv-tool/src/lib.rs, and uv-build-frontend/src/lib.rs. Both chosen snippets appear verbatim, reachable by BOTH arms: raw source lines near the call sites (bash's plain-text grep) and within a locate-style body excerpt of the caller's definition (vectr's locate-on-traced-caller-name followup).

must_contain_any: pub fn install_wheel<Cache: serde::Serialize, Build: serde::Serialize>(
notes: Two-hop chain confirmed real: download_wheel (uv-distribution/src/distribution_database.rs:872-873 onward) fetches the wheel bytes over HTTP into the cache and unzips them; install_wheel (uv-install-wheel/src/install.rs:21-29 onward) then unpacks the cached wheel's RECORD/dist-info into the venv's site-packages, the natural next hop. FIDELITY FIX (caught by actually running the pre-registered recipe, per rule (d)): the doc comments '/// Download a wheel from a URL...' and '/// Install the given wheel...' sit ABOVE the grep-matched fn lines, and T13's bash_recipe has no -A/-B context flags (unlike django T13's -A30/-A40), so those doc-comment lines were never bash-reachable -- a structural consequence of rustfmt wrapping multi-parameter signatures across lines, unlike Python's single-line def. Replaced with the exact signature lines the plain grep actually prints, both confirmed globally unique across crates/ and reachable by both arms (grep's raw output line and a locate/search chunk spanning the definition). distribution_database.rs's bare 'fn download' grep also matches 3 sibling functions in the same file (download_and_build, download_and_build_metadata, download_archive) -- noise, kept as-is per the honesty protocol, same flavor as django T13's 'def clean' over-match in fields.py.

must_contain_any: Either a built distribution, a wheel, or a source distribution that exists at some location.
notes: Real enum confirmed with exactly the shape guessed: Dist::Built(BuiltDist) / Dist::Source(SourceDist), where BuiltDist further splits into Registry/DirectUrl/Path and SourceDist into Registry/DirectUrl/GitDirectory/GitPath/Path/Directory -- matching the query's 'built wheel vs source distribution vs git vs local path' framing exactly. FILE-PATH DISCREPANCY for sentinel: the crate guess (uv-distribution-types) was correct but the file guess (src/dist.rs) was wrong -- there is no dist.rs in this crate; Dist lives directly in src/lib.rs. Bash recipe step 2 ('grep -n -A30 "enum Dist" crates/uv-distribution-types/src/dist.rs') will fail with 'No such file or directory', a genuine dead end left as pre-registered. Step 1 (directory-scoped, no filename) still succeeds but over-matches on the 'enum Dist' substring prefix, also surfacing DistRef (lib.rs:206), DistributionId (id.rs:213), and DistErrorKind (dist_error.rs:24) -- realistic grep noise, not curated away.
